orpAssembly.command: orp mode breakdown granular-breakdown --topic "problem 848 | convergence assembly | Explain the 282 First-Failure Mechanism" --json

assembledPieces.claim: The earlier p443 split exposed the selected q97 square-obstruction child plus a remaining p443-unavailable complement.
assembledPieces.boundary: It does not close the p443-unavailable complement or the wider p4217 complement.

assembledPieces.claim: The local/free exact p151 route is ledgered as blocked on the selected q97 child.
assembledPieces.boundary: This is not a proof that p151 fails in principle.

assembledPieces.claim: The p479 reserve gives an exact squarefree representative repair for the selected q97 child.
assembledPieces.boundary: A representative repair is not a full availability or squarefree-hitting theorem over the q97 child.

assembledPieces.claim: The p479 endpoint formula is k_479(w) = (131596*w + 250) mod 229441; exactly 1140 residues are p479-available and 228301 residues are p479-unavailable.
assembledPieces.boundary: The split does not close the available residues, the unavailable complement, the q97 child, or the p4217 complement.

assembledPieces.claim: The first p479-available residue family w = 229441*z contains a deterministic 127^2 square-obstruction subprogression with roots 4391 and 15510.
assembledPieces.boundary: This only emits the first square-obstruction child; it does not justify q127 repair descent before a bulk or ranked handoff is selected.

jointTheoremClaimSupported.statement: For the selected p443/q97 child, p479 availability is now a finite residue partition modulo 479^2, and the first available residue family has a deterministic q127 square-obstruction child.

frontierComparison.interpretation: The increase is expected from exposing explicit children, but it is not global convergence. Another single-child descent is disallowed until a bulk cover, structural decomposition, or ranked transition is selected.

nextFiniteToken.rankDecreaseIfCompleted: The p479-available side is replaced by terminal square-obstruction subclasses plus an explicit survivor boundary, instead of an unbounded q-prime selector chain.

compressionCandidate.name: p479_available_residue_square_obstruction_cover
compressionCandidate.coveredFamily: All 1140 p479-available residue classes modulo 479^2 inside the selected p443/q97 child.
compressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: It tests the whole p479-available side at once and can absorb the q127 child plus q127-avoiding available families into one finite cover or one survivor packet.
compressionCandidate.whyNotSingleQ127Descent: Descending into the selected q127 child would handle one exposed child while leaving the other p479-available residues and the p479-unavailable complement unassembled.

oneNextAction.action: Run a finite bulk square-obstruction screen over all 1140 p479-available residue classes modulo 479^2; emit a cover packet if every available residue is square-obstructed by a recorded q^2 class, or emit a deterministic survivor-boundary packet naming the uncovered residues and next structural denominator.
oneNextAction.coveredFamily: The full p479-available residue partition of the selected p443/q97 child, not a single q127 branch.
oneNextAction.finiteDenominatorOrRankToken: 479^2 availability period with 1140 available residues; completion consumes the p443_q97_p479_available_residue_set token.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If the finite bulk screen does not cover all 1140 residues, the packet must name the exact survivor residue list, checked q-prime bound, and deterministic successor denominator before any selector descent.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The residue set has only 1140 classes, so a bulk screen is local/free and directly addresses the guard's growth tripwire; a q127-only repair would keep the sibling ladder pattern alive.
oneNextAction.completionRule: A bulk-cover packet closes or classifies all 1140 p479-available residues, or a survivor-boundary packet records the exact uncovered finite set and next structural handoff.

nextTheoremMove: Run a finite bulk square-obstruction cover over the 1140 p479-available residue classes; emit a full cover or exact survivor boundary before any q127 repair descent.
proofBoundary: This convergence assembly records the post-p479 split frontier and selects a guard-approved bulk handoff. It does not perform the bulk cover, close the q127 child, close q127-avoiding p479-available residues, close the p479-unavailable complement, close the q97 child, close the p443-unavailable complement, close the wider p4217 complement, prove collision-free matching, or decide Problem 848.
